ipcw 0.1.0
  • Get started
    • Single-event IPCW
    • Competing-risks IPCW
  • Reference

Single-event example dataset

Source: R/data.R
single_example_dat.Rd

A simulated dataset used in the single-event IPCW guided example. It represents a clinical scenario where the event of interest is progression-free survival, the binary treatment covariate x distinguishes two treatment arms, and W2 is an informative censoring covariate (analogous to an ancillary biomarker). Censoring depends on W2, making standard Kaplan-Meier and Cox analyses potentially biased.

data(single_example_dat)

Format

A tibble with 500 rows and 5 columns:

S

True (latent) event time.

t

Observed time (minimum of S and the censoring time).

delta

Event indicator: 1 = event occurred, 0 = censored.

x

Binary treatment indicator: 0 = chemotherapy, 1 = TKI.

W2

Continuous covariate that drives informative censoring (analogous to an ancillary biomarker).

Source

Generated by the authors via sim_data_SE() with set.seed(20240429) and default parameters.

Details

The dataset was generated with the following parameters:

  • \(n = 500\), \(\alpha = 0.05\), \(\sigma = 500\), \(\beta = \log(0.25)\), \(\lambda = 0.01\), \(\phi = -5\).

  • set.seed(20240429).

See the single-event vignette for the full data-generation code.

Contents

Developed by Emily Zabor, Anne Eaton.
